skill.name: observe-whatsapp
skill.description: Observe and troubleshoot WhatsApp in Kapso: debug message delivery, inspect webhook deliveries/retries, triage API errors, and run health checks. Use when investigating production issues, message failures, or webhook delivery problems.
skill.summary: Troubleshoot Kapso WhatsApp delivery, webhook, API log, and health issues.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network, and credential access patterns, plus a critical heuristic combination. Review found only SKILL.md was scanned, and the flagged commands are Markdown instructions rather than executable code in the audited package. The skill still carries medium operational risk because it asks users to provide a Kapso API key and run referenced Node scripts whose implementations are not present in the scanned files.

medium_findings.title: Credentialed Command-Based Diagnostics
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to set Kapso API environment variables and run Node commands for WhatsApp diagnostics. This appears operationally legitimate, but it can expose message, webhook, and account data if the referenced commands are untrusted or run in the wrong environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Markdown file clearly asks users to provide an API key and run diagnostic Node commands. The scanned file does not show malicious implementation code, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Referenced Implementations Not Present In Audited Package
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md file lists many script and reference paths in its file map, but the audited file structure contains only SKILL.md. The behavior of the referenced tooling cannot be verified from the scanned package.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file map explicitly references scripts and references, while the report file structure lists only SKILL.md. This is strong evidence of an incomplete or unverifiable package.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Finding Is Benign
low_findings.description: The static network finding points to the source repository URL in frontmatter. This is metadata and is not evidence of a network request or data exfiltration in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 contains a repository URL, not executable code. No evidence found that this URL receives credentials or runtime data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line is a plain-language skill description. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing code, or encryption implementation appears in the scanned SKILL.md content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner matched text in prose, not a crypto API call. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic use.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flags Are Diagnostic Instructions
low_findings.description: The flagged overview and health-check instructions are scoped to Kapso WhatsApp diagnostics. They are not host reconnaissance commands in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe application health checks, not operating system enumeration. The absence of shell reconnaissance commands lowers the likelihood of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: WhatsApp delivery and webhook failures can be hard to trace across messages, logs, and health checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for Kapso diagnostics.
actual_capabilities: Guides investigation of WhatsApp message delivery by phone number, message ID, or recipient number.
Points users to workflows for message errors, external API logs, and webhook delivery attempts.
Describes health-check steps for project overview and WhatsApp phone number status.
Identifies related Kapso skills for webhook setup and WhatsApp automation tasks.
Lists expected environment variables for connecting diagnostic commands to Kapso.
limitations: The audited package contains only SKILL.md, so referenced scripts are not verified here.
The skill requires a Kapso API base URL and API key before diagnostic commands can work.
It is focused on observation and troubleshooting, not webhook creation or automation setup.
It does not include remediation steps for every WhatsApp or Kapso API error.

use_cases.title: Investigate Failed Customer Messages
use_cases.description: Trace a failed WhatsApp message from recipient number to message details and related conversation context.

use_cases.title: Triage Webhook Delivery Incidents
use_cases.description: Review webhook delivery attempts and API logs during an outage or delayed notification incident.

use_cases.title: Check WhatsApp Account Health
use_cases.description: Run project and phone number health checks before escalating a WhatsApp delivery issue.

prompt_templates.title: Start Message Investigation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a single failed message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me investigate a WhatsApp message delivery issue in Kapso. Ask for the phone number ID, recipient number, and message ID before suggesting commands.

prompt_templates.title: Review Webhook Failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through checking Kapso webhook delivery attempts and related API logs for a WhatsApp incident.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Account Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a WhatsApp health-check plan for a Kapso phone number and explain what each result should help confirm.

prompt_templates.title: Build Incident Timeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build an incident timeline using Kapso message details, webhook deliveries, API logs, and health checks.

output_examples.input: A customer did not receive a WhatsApp message.
output: Confirm the phone number ID and recipient number.
List recent messages for that phone number ID.
Check whether the conversation exists for the recipient.
Review related message errors before escalating.

output_examples.input: Webhook retries increased after a deployment.
output: Compare webhook delivery attempts around the deployment time.
Review external API logs for matching failures.
Separate provider errors from application response errors.
Document retry timing, response codes, and affected event types.

output_examples.input: A WhatsApp phone number may be unhealthy.
output: Run the phone number health check.
Confirm whether failures affect one number or the whole project.
Collect findings for the Kapso or Meta support path.

best_practices: Use a least-privilege Kapso API key and avoid sharing command output that contains customer data.
Start with message details before broad log searches to reduce noise and data exposure.
Record timestamps, IDs, and observed statuses while building an incident timeline.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production API keys into chat or commit them to files.
Do not run referenced scripts from an unverified source without reviewing their contents.
Do not use this skill for webhook setup, template creation, or automation changes.

faq.question: What does this skill help troubleshoot?
faq.answer: It helps investigate Kapso WhatsApp message delivery, webhook deliveries, API logs, and phone number health.

faq.question: Does it send WhatsApp messages?
faq.answer: No. The documented scope is observation and diagnostics, not sending messages or changing templates.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: The skill documentation expects a Kapso API base URL and a Kapso API key.

faq.question: Are the referenced scripts verified in this audit?
faq.answer: No. The audited package contains only SKILL.md, so referenced script implementations were not available for review.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose webhook setup problems?
faq.answer: It can inspect webhook deliveries and retries. The document points setup and signature verification tasks to integrate-whatsapp.

faq.question: Is this safe for production incidents?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow may access sensitive production data, so use least-privilege credentials and limit shared outputs.
